description: Detection evals: each fixture is a set of DevDigest backend files under pre-PR review with boundary violations planted (no marker comments). A run passes an assertion when its written review names that specific violation and, where relevant, does NOT flag the clean code alongside it.

evals.name: route-db-leak
evals.prompt: I'm adding a `pulls` module to the DevDigest server. This is `server/src/modules/pulls/routes.ts` before I open a PR — there's no service or repository yet, it's all in the route. Review it for architecture / layering problems and tell me what to change.
evals.expected_output: Flags that the HTTP/transport ring (routes.ts) imports drizzle-orm + db/client + db/schema and runs SELECT/INSERT inline; says routes must delegate; recommends moving the queries into a repository called from a service.

assertions.text: Identifies that routes.ts importing drizzle-orm / db/client / db/schema is a boundary violation (DB in the HTTP ring)
Flags the raw SELECT/INSERT queries running directly inside the route handlers
Recommends moving persistence into a repository and delegating from a service (routes stay thin)
References the onion rule/check by name (e.g. routes-no-db) or points at the dependency-cruiser / arch:check

evals.name: service-constructs-adapter
evals.prompt: Review `server/src/modules/repos/service.ts` for me before I push. Anything wrong with how it uses git and the LLM?
evals.expected_output: Flags that the application ring constructs concrete adapters (new SimpleGitClient, new OpenAIProvider) instead of resolving them from the DI Container (container.git, await container.llm('openai')); depend on the interface, not the impl. Should NOT claim `new RepoRepository(container.db)` is wrong — that is the allowed pattern.

assertions.text: Flags that the service constructs concrete adapters with `new` (SimpleGitClient and/or OpenAIProvider)
Recommends resolving adapters via the DI Container (container.git / container.llm) — depend on the interface, not the implementation
Notes the external SDK / secret leaking into the application ring (openai, simple-git, or process.env for keys)
Does NOT wrongly flag `new RepoRepository(container.db)` — a service constructing its own repository is the allowed pattern

evals.name: core-impurity
evals.prompt: This is `reviewer-core/src/review/run.ts`. I added persistence and a GitHub comment right where the review is produced so callers don't have to. Does this fit reviewer-core?
evals.expected_output: Flags that reviewer-core must stay pure: importing postgres (and running SQL) and octokit (and posting a comment) breaks core purity. Persistence and GitHub posting belong in the caller (server persists + streams; the CI runner posts); the core should take inputs and return data.

assertions.text: Flags reviewer-core importing postgres and running SQL as a purity violation
Flags reviewer-core importing octokit and posting a GitHub comment as a purity violation
Explains the core must return data and let the CALLER (server / CI runner) do persistence + GitHub posting
Does NOT flag the injected LLMProvider call or the pure prompt/grounding/score logic as wrong

evals.name: impure-helper-with-clean-distractor
evals.prompt: Two files from `server/src/modules/agents/` — `helpers.ts` and `routes.ts`. Review them for any layering issues before I open the PR.
evals.expected_output: Flags that helpers.ts is impure — resolveDefaultModel imports db/client + drizzle and runs a query; helpers must stay pure (that query belongs in a repository/service). Should NOT flag the pure toAgentDto mapper, the AGENT_JOB_KIND constant, or the clean routes.ts (which correctly delegates to a service).

assertions.text: Flags that helpers.ts is impure — resolveDefaultModel imports db/client + drizzle and runs a query
Recommends the query move to a repository/service so helpers stay pure
Does NOT flag the pure toAgentDto DTO mapper or the AGENT_JOB_KIND constant
Does NOT flag routes.ts — it correctly delegates to a service with no DB/SDK imports

evals.name: cross-module-repository-reach
evals.prompt: Review `server/src/modules/reviews/service.ts` before I open the PR. The reviews module needs some repo metadata to build the diff — is the way I'm getting it okay?
evals.expected_output: Flags that the reviews service imports and uses another module's repository (RepoRepository from ../repos/repository.js) — reaching into the repos slice's private infra ring. A module's public seam is its service; the fix is to call the repos module's service (or a shared @devdigest/shared port), not its repository. Must NOT flag `new ReviewRepository(container.db)` (own-module repository, allowed), the container.git / container.llm usage, or the reviewer-core call.

assertions.text: Flags that the reviews service reaches into ANOTHER module's repository (RepoRepository from ../repos) — cross-module infra coupling
Recommends going through the repos module's service (its public seam) or a shared port instead of importing its repository
Does NOT flag `new ReviewRepository(container.db)` — using the module's OWN repository is the allowed pattern
Does NOT flag the container.git / container.llm resolution or the reviewer-core reviewPullRequest call
Treats it as a hard, blocking violation (CRITICAL) rather than a soft suggestion/nit
Ties it to a mechanical rule the dependency-cruiser / arch:check enforces (rather than saying it passes arch:check and only a human can catch it)

evals.name: laundered-db-in-presenter
evals.prompt: Here's the whole `server/src/modules/runs/` module before I open the PR — routes.ts, service.ts, presenter.ts, repository.ts, constants.ts. The routes look thin and everything goes through the service; can you review the module for any layering / boundary problems?
evals.expected_output: Flags that presenter.ts runs Drizzle queries (imports drizzle-orm + db/schema) even though it is not a repository — laundered I/O. The route and service read clean, but getRunView passes container.db into the presenter, so the DB is reached transitively through a non-repository file. Fix: move the enrichment query into RunRepository (a join / method) and have the presenter map pure data. Must NOT flag repository.ts (which legitimately imports drizzle-orm + db/schema — it IS the repository), nor the pure constants.ts, nor the clean routes.ts.

assertions.text: Flags that presenter.ts imports the DB toolkit (drizzle-orm + db/schema) and runs queries despite not being a repository — misplaced/laundered I/O
Traces the leak across files — recognises the clean-looking route/service reach the DB transitively because getRunView passes container.db into the presenter
Recommends moving the enrichment query into RunRepository (repository ring) and keeping the presenter a pure mapper
Does NOT flag repository.ts — importing drizzle-orm + db/schema there is correct (it IS the repository ring)
Does NOT flag the pure constants.ts or the clean routes.ts as violations
